name: Horatio Alger Canadian Scholarship (Undergraduate)
description: Need-based scholarship support for full-time grade 11 high school students (or grade 12 in Quebec) from families with income under $80,000. Recipients must demonstrate integrity, perseverance in overcoming adversity, a commitment to post-secondary education, a desire to contribute to society, and a good academic record.

name: Vocational & Technical Scholarships
description: Scholarships awarded to individuals pursuing a certificate or diploma program. These awards aim to help students achieve their career aspirations and fulfill their potential in vocational and technical fields.

name: Graduate Scholarships
description: Scholarships for students who have completed a bachelor’s degree or higher and plan to pursue a master’s, PHD, or postdoctoral degree.

name: RBC Ignite Scholarship
description: Awards 500 scholarships annually to youth aged 16-20 who are a skill away from a great job. It provides financial support for courses, certifications, or training that are 12 months or less in duration, aiming to help youth access new skills.

name: RBC Elevate Scholarship
description: Awards 40 scholarships annually to individuals aged 17-29 aspiring to attend or currently attending post-secondary education in Canada. It aims to help individuals overcome financial barriers to pursue a recognized degree, certificate, or diploma from an accredited institution.

name: RBC Reimagine Scholarship
description: Provides 100 scholarships annually for adult learners (25 years or older, out of school for 3+ years) seeking upskilling or reskilling opportunities. It supports formal credentialed programs, career certifications, and micro-credentials to help individuals advance or transition careers.

name: Indspire Building Brighter Futures Bursary supported by RBC
description: A renewable bursary program for First Nation, Inuit, and Métis students across Canada. It provides financial support for both full-time and part-time studies in various fields including college, university, skilled trades, apprenticeships, and technology programs.

name: Building Brighter Futures: Bursaries and Scholarships
description: Indspire's flagship program offers financial support through bursaries and scholarships to First Nations, Inuit, and Métis students pursuing post-secondary education. It aims to open doors through education, helping cover costs such as tuition, childcare, food, shelter, travel, and cultural support.

name: Feiga Bresver Academic Fund
description: This fund is part of Indspire's Building Brighter Futures program, providing financial support to First Nations, Inuit, and Métis students pursuing post-secondary education across Canada. It helps cover various educational and living expenses.

name: Hilary Pearson Award
description: This award is part of Indspire's Building Brighter Futures program, providing financial support to First Nations, Inuit, and Métis students pursuing post-secondary education across Canada. It helps cover various educational and living expenses.

name: Indigenous Women Leadership Scholarship
description: This scholarship is part of Indspire's Building Brighter Futures program, specifically designed to support Indigenous women who demonstrate leadership qualities in their post-secondary education journey. It helps cover various educational and living expenses.

name: Len Flett Bursary
description: This bursary is part of Indspire's Building Brighter Futures program, providing financial support to First Nations, Inuit, and Métis students pursuing post-secondary education across Canada. It helps cover various educational and living expenses.

name: The Gale and Paul Taylor Family Bursary
description: This bursary is part of Indspire's Building Brighter Futures program, providing financial support to First Nations, Inuit, and Métis students pursuing post-secondary education across Canada. It helps cover various educational and living expenses.

name: Entrance Bursary Program
description: Designed to provide financial support to newly admitted students pursuing an Undergraduate Bachelor's degree. Eligibility is based on financial need, academic ranking from admission, and personal statements.

name: In-Course Bursary Program
description: Offers need-based funding to returning Bachelor's students in the Fall semester. Awards are made following an application-based competition that includes a financial need test and committee review.

name: Small grants (Project-based funding)
description: Provides financial support on a competitive basis for full- or part-time students undertaking special projects. Details on eligibility are available in the online bursary application system.

name: Funding for your international project (Travel-based funding)
description: Travel-based funding to help students finance international exchanges, internships, research abroad, or short-term courses. Further details are accessible through the online bursary application system.

name: Bourses Perspective Québec
description: An incentive scholarship program from The Government of Quebec aimed at supporting students in essential public services and strategic areas of the economy.

name: CSU Bursaries
description: Financial need-based funding to alleviate the high cost of post-secondary education, also recognizing academic and extra-curricular achievements.

name: Joyce Family Foundation
description: Provides support to students from rural areas, fostering their academic and professional growth through mentorship and internship opportunities.

name: High School Prestige Awards
description: These awards are designed for high-achieving high school students applying for admission to the University of Calgary. Eligibility is based on academic excellence and significant accomplishments outside the classroom.

name: President's Admission Scholarship
description: This scholarship is an automatic admission award offered by the University of Calgary. Specific details on its criteria are not provided in the text, but it falls under automatic awards for entering students.

name: High School Entrance Awards and Bursaries
description: These awards and bursaries are available to high school applicants seeking admission to the University of Calgary. An application is required, with a deadline in March.

name: Pathways to Medicine Scholarship
description: This scholarship is for students interested in medical pathways at the University of Calgary. Consideration requires submitting the general High School Awards application by December 1.

name: Athletic Awards
description: These awards recognize student-athletes at the University of Calgary. Recipients are selected through a nomination process by the university's athletics director, rather than requiring an application.

name: First Nations and Indigenous Black (FNIB) Scholarships
description: These entrance scholarships are specifically designed for students who identify as First Nations or Indigenous Black. Transfer students, who are generally ineligible for other entrance awards, are an exception and encouraged to apply for FNIB scholarships using the General Entrance Award application.

name: Dalhousie In-Course Scholarships
description: These merit-based scholarships are awarded to current students based on their academic achievement during their studies at Dalhousie. Transfer students become eligible for automatic assessment for these scholarships once they have successfully completed 30 credit hours over two academic semesters.

name: Schulich Leader Scholarships
description: The Schulich Leader Scholarships are listed as prestigious awards available to prospective students at Dalhousie University. These are highly competitive scholarships for exceptional high school graduates.

name: Bursaries for Transfer Students
description: Bursaries are a form of financial aid, typically awarded based on demonstrated financial need rather than academic merit. Transfer students at Dalhousie University are specifically eligible to apply for these bursaries to help with their educational costs.

name: General Entrance Awards
description: These awards are typically designed for new students entering Dalhousie University directly from high school. While generally available based on academic merit, transfer students are largely not eligible for these awards, with specific exceptions like the FNIB Scholarships.
